core.appIs: tic-tac-toe — a two-player m,n,k (3,3,3) game served as a web arena
core.goal: place three of your own marks in a row (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal); otherwise draw
core.howToJoin: take a seat by submitting the first move for a side; the first mover on each side is seated (X moves first, then O); later arrivals are observers

myAffordances: make a move on X's turn to an empty square

expectedPositionTokenSource.note: A=1 gates the active form, so an unseated agent is pushed toward /profile (haiku reported profile); board labels remain a valid source too.

expectedArtifactDiscovery: the /profile contract exists (advertised via the Link: rel=profile response header) and describes the make-move affordance
notes: A=1 gates the body move forms (seat+turn), so an unseated arrival sees no form at the bootstrap moment — same blind spot as 1000. But Sd exposes the /profile contract, which supplies the move format without a visible form. So the seat-taking move source is profile (matches haiku 1010: positionTokenSource=profile, bootstrap success). Steady-state, once seated, forms appear (board); profile remains available. The contrast 1000 vs 1010 isolates Sd's bootstrap-enabling effect under A=1.
